answer.title: Authority
answer.shortDefinition: Authority is recognized standing to direct, decide, or govern within a governance order.
answer.coreMeaning: Within the governance domain, authority functions by attaching directive right to a role, office, or position that others treat as operative. It concerns who may direct, not what can be enforced or whether that standing is valid.
answer.fullDefinition:
Authority is not any ability to produce effects. In ChatPDM v1 it is defined within the governance domain as a structured right to issue direction, make decisions, or govern within a governance order.

Its internal composition joins a directive claim to a recognized role, office, or position. The concept requires that the right to direct is treated as attached to standing rather than to mere capacity.

Within governance structures, authority organizes who may decide, command, or settle matters. Questions of whether that standing is valid belong to legitimacy, while questions of what outcomes can be produced belong to power.

sources.label: Oxford Languages
sources.usedFor: definition boundary for recognized right to direct or decide

sources.label: Max Weber, Economy and Society
sources.usedFor: structural distinction between recognized rule-bound command and mere power
